fields.title: Project Charter Fundamentals
fields.key_concepts:
• Understanding project charters
• Project authorization
• Stakeholder identification
• Scope definition
• Project objectives
fields.best_practices:
• Keep it concise
• Get stakeholder approval
• Define clear objectives
• Include success criteria
• Document assumptions

fields.title: Project Planning Basics
fields.key_concepts:
• Project planning fundamentals
• Work breakdown structure
• Resource allocation
• Timeline planning
• Risk identification
fields.best_practices:
• Define clear milestones
• Include all stakeholders

• Plan for risks
• Document assumptions

fields.text: What is the primary purpose of a project charter?
fields.explanation: A project charter formally authorizes the project and provides the project manager with the authority to apply organizational resources.

fields.text: Who typically approves the project charter?
fields.explanation: The project sponsor or senior management typically approves the project charter.

fields.text: What should be included in project success criteria?
fields.explanation: Success criteria should be specific, measurable, and aligned with business objectives.

fields.text: When should a project charter be created?
fields.explanation: A project charter should be created at the beginning of the project, before detailed planning begins.

fields.text: What role does the project manager play in creating the charter?
fields.explanation: The project manager typically assists in creating the charter but is not the one who authorizes it.

fields.text: To formally authorize the project and assign resources
fields.explanation: Correct! The project charter is the formal document that authorizes the project and assigns resources.

fields.text: To create a detailed project schedule
fields.explanation: Incorrect. Project schedules are created during project planning, not in the charter.
Incorrect. While the charter may include high-level deliverables, detailed deliverables are defined in the scope statement.
Correct! The project sponsor typically approves the project charter.
Incorrect. The project manager helps create the charter but doesn't approve it.

fields.text: Measurable outcomes aligned with business objectives
fields.explanation: Correct! Success criteria must be measurable and aligned with business goals.
Incorrect. Technical specifications are part of project planning, not success criteria.
Correct! The charter should be created during project initiation.
Incorrect. The charter needs to be in place before execution begins.

fields.text: Contributes to creation but doesn't authorize
fields.explanation: Correct! The PM helps create the charter but doesn't authorize it.
Incorrect. The project sponsor, not the PM, approves the charter.
